title: Conjecture 1.1 (Coarse Menger Conjecture — strong form)
statement_text: For all integers $k,c\geq 1$ there exists $\ell>0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be a graph and let $S,T\subseteq V(G)$. Then either there are $k$ paths between $S,T$, pairwise at distance at least $c$; or there is a set $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\leq k-1$ such that every path between $S,T$ contains a vertex with distance at most $\ell$ from some member of $X$.
context_text: This is the most natural coarse analogue of Menger's theorem, conjectured independently by two groups in 2023. Both groups proved the result for $k=2$. The authors of the current paper disproved it for all $k\geq 3$ (even with $c=3$ and maximum degree three) in their prior paper [9].
attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe, and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
paper_contribution: The authors established in their prior work [9] that this conjecture is false for all $k \geq 3$, even when $c = 3$ and $G$ has maximum degree three; the present paper then addresses the weaker Conjecture 1.2.
notes: The conjecture is labelled 'False conjecture' in the paper. Both attributing groups' arXiv preprints appeared in May 2023; the Albrechtsen et al. journal publication is 2024.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Weak Coarse Menger Conjecture)
statement_text: For all integers $k,c\geq 1$ there exist $m,\ell>0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be a graph and let $S,T\subseteq V(G)$. Then either there are $k$ paths between $S,T$, pairwise at distance at least $c$; or there is a set $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\leq m$ such that every path between $S,T$ contains a vertex with distance at most $\ell$ from some member of $X$.
context_text: After the strong Conjecture 1.1 was disproved, this weaker statement (allowing $|X|$ to depend on both $k$ and $c$ rather than being bounded by $k-1$) remained a popular open question. The authors themselves proposed it in their earlier paper [9] as the next most natural coarse analogue of Menger's theorem.
paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by constructing, for all integers $\ell, m \geq 1$, a graph $G$ and sets $S, T \subseteq V(G)$ witnessing its failure when $c = k = 3$ (Theorem 1.3); a variant of the counterexample has only one vertex of degree more than three.
notes: Labelled 'False conjecture' in the paper. Proposed in the authors' prior paper arXiv:2401.06685, published as J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 173 (2025).
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture

title: Open case: Coarse Menger Conjecture for c=2
statement_text: It is unknown whether Conjecture 1.1 holds when $c = 2$: for all integers $k \geq 1$ there exists $\ell > 0$ such that for every graph $G$ and $S, T \subseteq V(G)$, either there are $k$ paths between $S, T$ pairwise at distance at least $2$, or there is a set $X \subseteq V(G)$ with $|X| \leq k-1$ such that every $S$-$T$ path contains a vertex within distance $\ell$ of some member of $X$.
context_text: The authors note that the case $c = 3$ is of special interest because a positive result for $c = 3$ would imply one for all $c \geq 3$ (via taking the $c$-th power of $G$). They explicitly remark that 'the conjecture remains open when $c = 2$, and we have nothing to say about that case in this paper.'
notes: Stated as a passing remark in the introduction; no formal conjecture label. Refers specifically to the strong-form Conjecture 1.1 restricted to c=2.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. IV. A counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture
